title: Language-Specific Prompting
concept: Customizing instruction framing based on the target language to optimize compile-readiness.

exercises.prompt: What is a common reason for code models to output '// TODO' or cut off their responses?

options.text: They don't understand the prompt well enough.
They are programmed to finish every response.
They only output full solutions when told to.

exercises.explain: The models often cut off or leave placeholders like '// TODO' because they may not fully grasp the instructions given in the prompt.

cards.title: Introduction to Language-Specific Prompting
cards.body: Language-Specific Prompting is about tailoring your instructions so that they fit the programming language you're using. This helps models produce clearer and more complete code.

cards.title: What is Language-Specific Prompting?
cards.body: It involves customizing how you ask the AI to generate code based on which programming language you use. This increases the chances of the output being ready to compile.

cards.title: Analogy: Language Learning
cards.body: Just like a student learns different things in Spanish versus French, programming models also need specific guiding words for different coding languages.

cards.title: Example of Language-Specific Prompting
cards.body: Instead of saying 'Write a function', you might say 'In Python, write a function that sorts a list.' This gives clarity on the language and task.

cards.title: Myth: Any Prompt Works for Any Language
cards.body: It's a myth that one-size-fits-all prompts will yield successful outputs. Specific languages require specific prompts for best results.

cards.title: Language-Specific Prompts Comparison
cards.body: Compare the clarity of prompts for different languages.

left.text: Write a function to calculate the factorial of a number.

right.text: Create a method to calculate factorial.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to customize prompts based on the target language?

options.text: To ensure the model produces usable code.
Because all programming languages are the same.
To confuse the AI model deliberately.
To reduce the length of the output.

exercises.explain: Customizing prompts for the target language leads to more accurate and usable code outputs from the model.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A generic prompt will always yield the best results regardless of programming language.
exercises.explain: Generic prompts often do not account for the specifics of the programming language, thus leading to less accurate responses.

exercises.prompt: To optimize code generation, you should customize your instructions based on the target ______.
exercises.sentence: To optimize code generation, you should customize your instructions based on the target language.
exercises.explain: Using 'language' specifies that we need to adapt our instructions to different programming dialects.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following prompts is better for generating JavaScript code?

options.text: Create a variable with a string value.
In JavaScript, create a function that reverses a string.

exercises.explain: The second option is clearer because it specifies the language (JavaScript) and the action (create a function).

exercises.prompt: Which custom instruction would most likely yield the best code generation for Ruby?

options.text: Generate a function to sort items.
In Ruby, create a method that sorts an array in ascending order.
Write some code to manage lists.

exercises.explain: The second option is optimal because it clearly specifies the language and the specific task, making it easier for the model to generate effective code.
